title: The Midnight Breach
instruction: Analyze the live firewall logs below. Click on the request that indicates a Directory Traversal attack.
explanation: The request containing '../../../etc/passwd' is a classic Directory Traversal attack attempting to access sensitive Linux system files outside the web folder.

title: Database Probe
instruction: Analyze the server logs. Click the request that indicates a SQL Injection attempt.
explanation: The '1 OR 1=1' condition is a classic SQL Injection attempt used to bypass database queries.

title: Injected Comment
instruction: Identify the request that contains a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) payload.
explanation: The script tag indicates an XSS payload attempting to execute JavaScript in the browser.

title: System Command Attack
instruction: Find the request attempting command injection.
explanation: The ';cat /etc/passwd' indicates command chaining used in command injection attacks.

title: Hidden Files
instruction: Which log shows a Directory Traversal attempt?
explanation: The '../' path is used to access files outside the intended directory.

title: User Data Exposure
instruction: Find the request demonstrating an IDOR attack.
explanation: Accessing sequential user IDs may indicate IDOR attempts to retrieve unauthorized user data.

title: Brute Force Attempt
instruction: Find the login request that indicates brute-force activity.
explanation: Multiple rapid login failures from the same IP indicate a brute-force attack.

title: Encoded Attack
instruction: Identify the log containing an encoded XSS payload.
explanation: The encoded '<script>' tag (%3Cscript%3E) indicates a hidden XSS attack.

title: Config Exposure
instruction: Find the request attempting to access a sensitive configuration file.
explanation: The '.env' file usually contains sensitive credentials and should never be publicly accessible.

title: Suspicious Redirect
instruction: Identify the request attempting an open redirect.
explanation: Attackers exploit open redirects to send users to malicious websites.

title: Unauthorized API Access
instruction: Find the request attempting to access restricted API data.
explanation: The attacker attempted to access an admin-only API endpoint.
